data.title: Macquarie Expects Japan to Consider FX Intervention Near 165 in USD/JPY
data.description:
Macquarie Group said Japan’s authorities may be preparing for a new round of foreign-exchange intervention, but could wait until the U.S. dollar rises to around 165 yen.

According to Jin10, the bank also said any intervention in the FX market may be delayed until after the release of today’s U.S. nonfarm payrolls data.

Macquarie said weaker-than-expected payrolls data could create the best conditions for intervention and maximize its effectiveness. It added that intervening before the data, followed by a strong payrolls report that lifts the dollar broadly, would amount to wasting “ammunition.”

data.title: KKR-Backed Musinsa Expands Stores in China and Japan Ahead of IPO
data.description: Musinsa Co., the KKR & Co.-backed South Korean fashion retailer, is accelerating its physical store expansion in China and Japan as it seeks to challenge Uniqlo and build momentum ahead of a closely watched initial public offering, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Meta Held Talks to Buy Kalshi but Chose to Build Arena Prediction App Instead
data.description: Meta previously held talks about acquiring prediction-market company Kalshi but ultimately decided to develop its own prediction-market app called Arena, which uses virtual currency rather than real-money wagers, according to 36Kr. The report said Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg met Kalshi CEO Tarek Mansour last year when Kalshi’s popularity was surging to discuss a potential acquisition, but the talks did not progress. Accounts differ on why negotiations broke down, with one version saying Mansour was unwilling to sell and another saying Meta viewed Kalshi’s legal and ethical issues as too complex.

data.title: ECB Governing Council Member Kazaks Says One More Rate Hike Is a Reasonable Expectation
data.description: ECB Governing Council member Martins Kazaks said it is a reasonable expectation that the European Central Bank will raise interest rates one more time, according to 36Kr.

data.title: AI TRENDS | White House Speeds Up Plan for Voluntary Standards on AI Model Releases
data.description:
The White House is accelerating efforts to set voluntary standards for the release of new artificial intelligence models, the Financial Times reported.

According to Jin10, the U.S. government has been in detailed talks with AI companies to develop the standards after it became involved in the deployment of frontier tools from firms including Anthropic and OpenAI.

People familiar with the discussions said the standards could be announced as soon as next week. They are expected to set benchmarks for models with advanced cyber capabilities and establish a release timetable aimed at streamlining future product launches.

The move is intended to implement an AI executive order issued last month by U.S. President Donald Trump, as groups involved seek clarity on how the government will regulate the technology.

One person familiar with White House discussions said the U.S. AI Standards and Innovation Center and the National Security Agency will play key roles in developing and overseeing the standards.

Sources also said U.S. officials will clarify which entities can access advanced models inside and outside the United States, a step that could lay the groundwork for a global framework that includes U.S. allies.

data.title: James Wynn Liquidated Twice in 24 Hours for $982,000 on S&P 500 Perpetual Shorts
data.description: Trader James Wynn was liquidated twice in 24 hours for a total of $982,000 after shorting S&P 500 perpetual futures. On-chain intelligence firm Arkham flagged the losses on July 1, according to BeInCrypto, and said another 0.35% rise in the index would liquidate his remaining position. Wynn has stayed on Hyperliquid, shifting from Bitcoin (BTC) and meme coin bets to leveraged index perpetuals. The latest losses push his career tally past 200 liquidations.

data.title: SpaceX Developed Phone-Like Device Prototype for AI Interaction, Report Says
data.description:
SpaceX has developed a prototype of a phone-like device intended to change how people interact with artificial intelligence, according to a Wall Street Journal report citing sources.

According to Odaily, the report said SpaceX showed the prototype to some investors ahead of an initial public offering.

Elon Musk later denied the report.

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. AI Guidelines Expected as Soon as Next Week After Model Release Intervention
data.description:
U.S. AI guidelines are expected to be announced as soon as next week following U.S. government intervention in Anthropic and OpenAI’s model release plans.

According to Jin10, the Financial Times reported that the move came after the U.S. government intervened in the companies’ plans for releasing their models.

The report said the guidelines could be published as early as next week.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Analyst Jukan Says MediaTek Has Likely Secured a Second ASIC Customer, Reportedly Meta
data.description:
MediaTek has largely confirmed a second ASIC customer beyond Google, and the customer is expected to be Meta, according to supply chain information cited by Citrini analyst Jukan in a post on X.

According to Odaily, MediaTek typically does not publicly comment on individual products, customers, or market rumors.

The report said Qualcomm appears to currently hold key customers including Meta, Microsoft, and ByteDance, but industry sources believe MediaTek is not at a disadvantage as it continues to deepen cooperation with Google and may secure a second customer.

Supply chain sources said that among major cloud service providers, Google is viewed as the most proactive customer in ordering AI data center and ASIC products and in driving generational transitions.

The report added that MediaTek has two products codenamed Zebrafish and Humufish, and market information and ASIC industry sources said its participation in the v9-generation Triggerfish is nearly certain. This would mean MediaTek could receive stable revenue contributions from TPU mass production from late 2026 through 2028, and potentially extending to 2029.

The report compared this with Qualcomm’s stated 2029 cloud AI revenue target of $15 billion, adding that industry sources see MediaTek reaching a $10 billion level as a matter of time if it holds multiple TPU ASIC orders.

Industry attention is also focused on whether MediaTek can secure a second major cloud service provider customer. The report said earlier market information and recent supply chain confirmation indicate MediaTek is still actively working with Meta on ASIC products, likely centered on AI accelerator chips.

Meta has recently reached partnerships with Arm and Qualcomm, but the report said those products are aimed at CPUs, with no clearer cooperation disclosed for self-developed AI accelerator chips.

IC design industry sources said Meta’s recent cloud AI strategy has been relatively unclear, with internal chip development plans adjusted multiple times and multiple partners sought for CPUs. They added that although Meta previously announced a cooperation plan with Broadcom, supply chain information indicates this has not halted the MediaTek-Meta cooperation plan.

The report said MediaTek and Qualcomm have different cloud AI approaches, with MediaTek concentrating resources on ASICs while Qualcomm plans to advance both customized and standardized products across AI accelerators and CPUs.

data.title: Deutsche Bank Says Tokenization Could Reshape Traditional Financial Markets
data.description:
Deutsche Bank said tokenization could reshape traditional financial markets by changing how banks manage liquidity and transact in short-term funding markets. According to NS3.AI, the bank said the technology could lower banks' reserve requirements and increase intraday repo transactions.

The comments follow Deutsche Bank’s earlier move into the sector. Last year, the bank announced plans to launch an early version of a real-world asset tokenization platform.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | SPDR Gold Trust Holdings Rise by 0.285 Tons to 1,005.362 Tons
data.description: Holdings in SPDR Gold Trust, the world’s largest gold ETF, increased by 0.285 tons from the previous day to 1,005.362 tons. According to Jin10, the latest update showed the fund’s total gold holdings at 1,005.362 tons.

data.title: Solana (SOL) Near $77 After 16% Weekly Bounce as Network Activity Nears Yearly Highs
data.description:
SOL traded near $77 after a 16% weekly bounce, but remained about 74% below its record high of $293. On-chain activity is climbing toward yearly highs, according to BeInCrypto, with active addresses retesting just below 7 million and seven-day average transactions per second trending toward 1,100.

Technically, SOL is defending the 0.786 Fibonacci retracement near $73, with resistance around $80 and then $120. BeInCrypto said a daily close above $80 could open a path toward $100 and $120, while a break below $73 could revisit $63; the Alpenglow consensus upgrade in the third quarter is cited as a potential catalyst.

data.title: STOCKS | US Stocks End Slightly Lower as Chipmakers Slide; Meta Jumps 8.8%
data.description:
US stocks finished slightly lower on Wednesday as technology shares fell, while gains in Meta Platforms helped limit declines in the S&P 500 and Nasdaq, according to RTHK.

Chipmakers were among the biggest drags, with an index of semiconductors ending 6.3% lower, and technology leading declines among S&P 500 sectors. Some market participants cited concerns about lofty valuations and heavy AI spending by tech companies.

Meta shares rallied 8.8% after Bloomberg News reported the company is building a cloud business to sell excess AI computing capacity. Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh also said inflation risks had eased recently.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 13 points, or 0.03%, to 52,305. The S&P 500 lost 16 points, or 0.2%, to 7,483, and the Nasdaq Composite dropped 173 points, or 0.7%, to 26,040.

Warsh said he would stick firmly to the US central bank's 2% inflation target and would "disappoint" anyone expecting loose monetary policy despite U.S. President Donald Trump's call for interest rate cuts. Traders slightly pared rate-hike expectations during his remarks but still expect at least one hike this year, according to LSEG data.

Investors also digested Institute for Supply Management data showing US manufacturing activity slowed in June but remained solid. Alcoa shares fell 8.9% after Australia's South32 agreed to sell most of its aluminium assets to Alcoa.

The key monthly US jobs report is due out on Thursday, with the market closed on Friday ahead of the Fourth of July holiday.

data.title: STOCKS | Asian Stocks Set to Fall as US Chipmaker Selloff Weighs, Traders Assess Warsh
data.description: Asian stocks were set to decline Thursday as a selloff in US chipmakers weighed on sentiment and investors assessed fresh remarks from Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 61,000 USDT with a 4.07% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jul 01, 2026, 22:23 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 61,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 61,098 USDT, with a narrowed 4.07% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: WORLD CUP | Tuchel: England won't have time to adapt to altitude for Mexico last-16
data.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel said his players will not have enough time to adapt to the high altitude in Mexico City before their World Cup last-16 match against Mexico at the Azteca Stadium, around 7,200ft above sea level. England booked the trip with Harry Kane's double against DR Congo and will play Mexico on Monday, 6 July at 01:00 BST; Mexico have played all four of their World Cup games at high altitude, according to Yahoo Sports.

data.title: Global Treasurers Drive Record $36 Billion in Kangaroo Bond Sales
data.description:
Australia’s bond market is drawing increasing interest from corporate treasurers worldwide, pushing kangaroo bond issuance to a record $36 billion, according to Bloomberg.

The surge has been supported by a flood of Asian capital that has helped cap borrowing costs and intensify competition for deals.

data.title: K Wave Media Sells Remaining 88 Bitcoin to Repay $6 Million Debt
data.description:
K Wave Media (KWM), a South Korea-listed company, has sold its remaining 88 BTC to repay $6 million in debt and now holds zero bitcoin.

According to Odaily, BitcoinTreasuries.NET said in a post on X that KWM disposed of all of its remaining bitcoin holdings as part of the debt repayment.

data.title: Lenders to Clearlake-Backed Tungsten Sign Pact After Debt Prices Slide
data.description: Lenders to Tungsten Automation have signed a pact agreeing to work together on a potential debt restructuring after a steep slide in the software company’s debt prices, according to Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Trump’s Nuclear Watchdog to Ditch 50-Year-Old Radiation Guidance
data.description: The US Nuclear Regulatory Commission plans to scrap guidance on radiation exposure for power plant workers that’s been in place since the mid-1970s, according to Bloomberg.
